header.title: C4 Dashboard
settings.title: Settings
settings.description: Customize the dashboard layout and appearance. Preferences are stored in this browser.
login.title: C4 Sign in
login.description: Session required to access the dashboard.
worker.action.close.confirm: Close worker "{name}"? This will terminate the session.
planPage.title: Plan
planPage.description: Dispatch a planning task and render the worker's plan.md. Re-dispatch the plan as real work with one click.
planPage.formDescription: Pick a worker, optionally pin a branch + output path, then describe the work.
scribePage.title: Scribe
scribePage.description: Session context recorder. Tail activity into docs/session-context.md so the next manager session picks up where the last one left off.
swarmPage.title: Swarm
swarmPage.description: Given a root worker, show the hierarchy of sub-workers it spawned and each node's status.
batchPage.title: Batch dispatch
batchPage.description: Send the same task to N workers or one task per line from a pasted list. Mirrors `c4 batch`.
batchPage.task.placeholder: Task to send to every worker
tokenUsagePage.title: Token usage
tokenUsagePage.description: Per-worker and per-day token consumption, plus the current tier quota snapshot.
tokenUsagePage.byDay.empty: No per-day usage recorded in this window.
morningPage.title: Morning report
morningPage.description: Daily overview — yesterday's activity, open TODOs, token spend. Mirrors `c4 morning`.
autoPage.title: Auto mode
autoPage.description: Spawn an autonomous manager + scribe pair. The manager creates its own workers, dispatches work, and reports back on completion.
autoPage.field.name: Manager name (optional)
autoPage.task.placeholder: Describe the outcome for the autonomous manager to deliver.
autoPage.result.heading: Dispatched
cleanupPage.title: Cleanup
cleanupPage.description: Remove orphan c4/ branches, worktrees, and directories left behind by crashed workers or prior runs. Always runs a dry-run first.
validationPage.title: Validation
validationPage.description: Per-worker validation object — tests / typecheck / lint status from .c4-validation.json in each worktree.
validationPage.filter.label: Filter workers
healthPage.title: Health
healthPage.description: Daemon heartbeat. Uptime, worker counts, queue depth, and a snapshot of loaded modules.
healthPage.refresh.label: Refresh health
healthPage.modules.empty: Loaded-modules / event-loop-lag fields are not yet exposed by the daemon. See TODO 8.20b-health-extensions.
templatesPage.title: Templates
templatesPage.description: Reusable worker templates — name, model, effort, profile, source.
templatesPage.filter.label: Filter templates
profilesPage.title: Profiles
profilesPage.description: Permission profiles — allow / deny patterns a worker inherits when spawned with --profile.
profilesPage.filter.label: Filter profiles
specialists.title: Specialists
specialists.search.placeholder: Search id / displayName / systemPrompt / domain / keywords (whitespace = AND)
specialists.search.label: Filter specialists
specialists.add.label: Add specialist
specialists.audit.heading: Audit log
specialists.confirmRemove.suffix: ? Score history is dropped from the persisted overlay; the seed entry stays.
specialists.empty.pick: Pick a specialist to see brain config + score history.
specialists.empty.scoreHistory: No score history yet — run + finalize a meeting that selects this specialist.
specialists.suggest.title: Suggested revision (review only)
specialists.suggest.empty: Brain returned no parseable revision. Try with a real claude brain.
specialists.suggest.applyHint: To apply via meeting consensus, click "Apply via meeting" above (or use
specialists.propose.accepted: accepted by {count} specialist(s) → added to registry (meeting {meetingId})
workerDetail.mergeConfirm:
Merge worker "{name}" into main?

This runs the pre-merge checks and performs git merge --no-ff.
meetings.empty.pick: Pick a meeting from the list to see its transcript.
meetings.empty.list: No meetings yet — `c4 meeting create "<task>"` to start one.
meetings.prune.daysInvalid: days must be a positive number
meetings.prune.confirm.header: Permanently prune meetings older than {days} day(s)?
meetings.prune.confirm.footer: Run dry-run first to see candidates.
specialists.json.label: Specialist JSON
specialists.tags.placeholder: comma-separated; prefix with + to add, - to remove
specialists.tooltip.export: Download a self-contained JSON bundle of the registry
specialists.tooltip.applyImport: Apply the bundle for real (governance event — audited)
specialists.tooltip.rotateAudit: Rotate the audit JSONL into a timestamped archive
specialists.tooltip.exportCsv: Download CSV of audit entries in the current window
specialists.tooltip.verifyChain: Verify the live audit-log hash chain
specialists.tooltip.verifyPlusRotated: Verify the live audit-log + rotated archives
specialists.tooltip.propose: POST to /specialists/propose — drives a meta-meeting and adds only on consensus
specialists.tooltip.underperform: Sustained negative retro score in at least one bucket
specialists.tooltip.scoreReset: Wipe score record (Phase 8.5). Audit log preserves the before-snapshot.
specialists.tooltip.suggest: Ask brain to draft a revised systemPrompt — review-only
specialists.tooltip.applyMeeting: Spawn meta-meeting and apply revision on consensus
meetings.title: Meetings
meetings.search.placeholder: Search transcripts… (FTS5: phrases in quotes, * for prefix)
meetings.maintenance.heading: Maintenance
meetings.fork.task.placeholder: task override (default: same as source — fill in when redoing with sharper scope)
meetings.compose.task.placeholder: Task description (e.g. "rotate auth secret in production")
meetings.tooltip.saveTemplate: Save the current task as a new template
meetings.tooltip.integrity: Run SQLite PRAGMA integrity_check on the persist DB
meetings.tooltip.backup: Hot backup via SQLite VACUUM INTO
meetings.tooltip.dryRun: Preview which meetings would be pruned
meetings.tooltip.prune: Permanently delete meetings older than N days
meetings.tooltip.contribute: Post a contribution from a specific specialist
meetings.tooltip.advance: Advance to the next stage if consensus is reached
meetings.tooltip.nextRound: Bump round counter on the current stage (refused past round cap)
meetings.tooltip.startManual: Mark the meeting as in-progress without running. For manual / CLI-driven sessions.
meetings.tooltip.peerRetro: Each speaker rates their peers; aggregate folds into the registry score
meetings.tooltip.retroPreview: Compute retro score deltas without applying
meetings.tooltip.finalize: Apply retro deltas to the registry score record
meetings.tooltip.fork: Clone this meeting as a new pending session — replan or reuse
meetings.publish.label: Publish meeting to wiki
meetings.peerRetro.label: Run peer retro
meetings.brain.label: brain:
meetings.peerBrain.label: peer brain:

workflows.empty: No workflows yet. Use {cli} to add one.
workflows.editor.selectNode: Select a node to inspect its config.
workflows.inputs.title: Provide JSON inputs for this run (default: empty object)

workflows.empty.selection: Select a workflow on the left to view its DAG.

wiki.tooltip.reopen: Spawn a new meeting seeded with this page + its related neighbours

riskPage.intro.suffix: CLI. Type a Bash command to see the classifier's verdict (level, action, matched rules, intent extraction). Stats panel below summarises denials + shadow-exec counts in the chosen window.

riskPage.sandboxPreview.tooltip: Show what argv the configured sandbox runtime would use — pure builder, no exec
controlPanel.lastBatch.title: Last batch results
sessions.tooltip.noLiveProcess: No running claude process owns this JSONL
config.title: Config
config.refresh.label: Refresh config
config.intro: Mirrors c4 config. The daemon sanitises secrets (slack tokens, JWT secret, etc.) before serving; what you see here is safe to copy / share. Use Reload after editing config.json on disk to apply without a daemon restart.
config.heading: Live config
config.filter.label: Filter config keys
config.reload.title: POST /api/config/reload — re-reads config.json, restarts subsystems as needed
riskPage.classify.heading: Classify a command
riskPage.command.label: Command to classify
riskPage.filter.label: Filter rule catalog
pinnedRules.role.label: Role template
pinnedRules.list.label: Persistent Rules
pinnedRules.title: Persistent Rules
pinnedRules.description: These rules are re-injected into the worker on a timer and after every auto-compact. Use {separator} on its own line to separate rules.
pinnedRules.roleField.label: Role template
pinnedRules.listField.label: Persistent Rules textarea
pinnedRules.placeholder:
Never run compound commands.

---

Commit to feature branch only.
rbac.title: RBAC
rbac.description: Role-based access control — read-only roster of roles and per-user grants.
rbac.refresh.label: Refresh RBAC
rbac.intro: Mirrors c4 rbac roles + c4 rbac users. Mutations (assign / grant / revoke) currently live only on the CLI. This view is for operators who need to confirm who has what without dropping to shell.
rbac.roles.heading: Roles
rbac.users.heading: Users ({n})
workspaces.title: Workspaces
workspaces.refresh.label: Refresh workspaces
workspaces.intro: Mirrors c4 workspaces. Workspaces are configured in config.workspaces; edit the daemon's config.json + reload to add or remove entries (Config page → Reload from disk).
workspaces.heading: Configured workspaces
workspaces.empty: No workspaces configured. Set config.workspaces as an array of {name, path}.
autonomous.title: Autonomous loop
autonomous.notEnabled: Autonomous mode is not enabled. Set config.autonomous.mode = true and restart the daemon to use this tab.

controlPanel.status.title: Status message
controlPanel.status.description: Post a short status update to Slack tagged with this worker. Used for oncall handoffs and incident notes.
controlPanel.worker.title: Control
controlPanel.worker.description: Actions for {worker}. Destructive actions prompt for confirmation; Pause / Resume fire immediately.
controlPanel.batch.title: Batch
controlPanel.batch.description: {count} selected - target multiple workers at once.
controlPanel.batch.confirmClose: Close {count} worker(s)? Each session and worktree will be terminated.
controlPanel.batch.confirmCancel: Cancel the current task for {count} worker(s)?
controlPanel.action.pause.label: Pause (Ctrl+C)
controlPanel.action.pause.description: Send Ctrl+C to interrupt the current work without terminating the session.
controlPanel.action.resume.label: Resume (Enter)
controlPanel.action.resume.description: Send Enter to resume an idle prompt or approve a pending confirmation.
controlPanel.action.cancel.label: Cancel task
controlPanel.action.cancel.description: Cancel the queued / pending / in-flight task for this worker.
controlPanel.action.cancel.confirm: Cancel the current or pending task for "{worker}"?
controlPanel.action.restart.label: Restart
controlPanel.action.restart.description: Kill the PTY process and spawn a fresh one on the same branch/worktree.
controlPanel.action.restart.confirm: Restart "{worker}"? The current process will be killed and a fresh session will start on the same branch.
controlPanel.action.rollback.label: Rollback
controlPanel.action.rollback.description: git reset --soft back to the worker start commit. Staged changes are preserved.
controlPanel.action.rollback.confirm: Rollback "{worker}" to its start commit? Commits made during the session will be unstaged.
controlPanel.action.close.label: Stop / Close
controlPanel.action.close.description: Terminate the worker, remove its worktree, and delete the c4/ branch.
controlPanel.action.close.confirm: Close "{worker}"? This terminates the session and removes its worktree and branch.

config.reloadConfirm:
Reload config.json from disk?

Sub-systems may restart. Use this when you've edited the
daemon config file and want it to take effect without a
full daemon restart.
wiki.bulkPublishConfirm:
Publish a wiki page for every terminal meeting without one?
Idempotent (existing pages are skipped). Use the CLI for force-overwrite.
specialists.audit.empty.window: No audit entries in the last {window}.
specialists.applyConfirm:
Apply revision via meta-meeting?

A new meeting fires immediately. If consensus is reached the
systemPrompt is replaced in the registry and an audit entry
is recorded. The meeting id will appear in the result.
specialists.import.governanceWarning: This is a governance event — recorded in the audit log.
meetings.escalateConfirm: Mark this meeting as escalated? (round cap or veto deadlock)
meetings.abortConfirm: Abort this meeting? Mutations refused after.

feature.workersHero.label: Workers
feature.workersHero.description: At-a-glance worker dashboard with busy/idle/lost counts, last-hour activity, and spawn CTA.
feature.sessions.label: Sessions
feature.sessions.description: Operator session log with active/archived/all tabs, debounce search, and detail drawer.
feature.scribe.label: Scribe
feature.scribe.description: Session context recorder — start/stop/scan and view snapshots.
feature.batch.label: Batch
feature.batch.description: Dispatch N workers with the same task or one per line.
feature.cleanup.label: Cleanup
feature.cleanup.description: Remove orphan worktrees and dead c4/ branches.
feature.swarm.label: Swarm
feature.swarm.description: Swarm tree and per-node status for a worker.
feature.tokenUsage.label: Token usage
feature.tokenUsage.description: Token consumption per worker / day with tier quota caps.
feature.plan.label: Plan
feature.plan.description: Dispatch a planner task and render the resulting plan.md.
feature.morning.label: Morning report
feature.morning.description: Generate the daily morning report — what happened, what is open.
feature.auto.label: Auto
feature.auto.description: Spawn an autonomous manager + scribe for a given task.
feature.queue.label: Queue
feature.queue.description: Edit the autonomous queue markdown -- reorder rows, flip status, edit detail.
feature.snapshots.label: Snapshots
feature.snapshots.description: Save and restore the daemon config plus autonomous queue markdown as timestamped snapshots.
feature.templates.label: Templates
feature.profiles.label: Profiles
feature.profiles.description: Permission profiles — allow/deny matrix for worker scopes.
feature.config.label: Config
feature.config.description: Live daemon config (sans secrets); reload from disk.
feature.designSystem.label: Design System
feature.designSystem.description: Live demos of UI primitives with variant matrices and code snippets.
feature.uiDemo.label: UI Demo
feature.uiDemo.description: Storybook-style gallery of every UI primitive for visual QA. Gated by the uiDemoRoute feature flag.
feature.decisionLog.label: Decision Log
feature.decisionLog.description: ADR-style log of architectural decisions -- context, decision, and consequences for each.
feature.commandHistory.label: Command History
feature.commandHistory.description: Local log of recent CommandPalette activations (last 50). Rerun per row, or clear all.
feature.workspaces.label: Workspaces
feature.rbac.label: RBAC
feature.rbac.description: Read-only roster of roles + per-user grants.
feature.notifications.label: Notifications
feature.notifications.description: Unified feed of lifecycle events (dispatch/complete/halt/escalation) from the autonomous loop.
feature.health.label: Health
feature.health.description: Daemon status, uptime, queue depth and loaded modules.
feature.uptime.label: Uptime
feature.uptime.description: Daemon process uptime, operator-local restart count, and the five most recent halt / escalation incidents.
feature.validation.label: Validation
feature.validation.description: Per-worker validation object — tests / typecheck / lint summary.
feature.risk.label: Risk
feature.risk.description: Preview a command's risk classification + recent denials in the last N hours.
feature.errorReports.label: Error Reports
feature.errorReports.description: Local in-memory feed of captured client errors (window / unhandledrejection / react / manual). Last 50 entries.
feature.keyboardMap.label: Keyboard Map
feature.keyboardMap.description: Full reference of keyboard bindings across the app -- navigation, actions, view, editing, global and custom shortcuts.
feature.keyboardRemap.label: Keyboard Remap
feature.keyboardRemap.description: Customise the keyboard shortcuts used across the app. Overrides are stored locally in this browser.
keyboardRemap.title: Keyboard Remap
keyboardRemap.description: Override the default keyboard shortcuts. Stored locally per browser under c4:bindings.
keyboardRemap.hint: Click Edit on a row and press the new key combo. Press Escape to cancel.
keyboardRemap.dialog.title: Edit binding
keyboardRemap.dialog.prompt: Press the new key combination. Modifiers + key, or a chord like "g h".
feature.featureFlags.label: Feature Flags
feature.featureFlags.description: Toggle component-scoped UI feature flags (grid overlay, route progress, page transitions, motion, reduced motion).
feature.settingsPage.label: Settings
feature.settingsPage.description: Consolidated landing for theme, locale, scribe, notifications, feature flags and a link to the daemon config.
keyboardMap.title: Keyboard Map
keyboardMap.description: Complete reference of every keyboard binding wired into the web UI.
keyboardMap.search.placeholder: Filter shortcuts by label or key...
keyboardMap.empty.title: No matching shortcuts
keyboardMap.empty.description: Adjust the search to find a binding.
keyboardMap.row.menuMoveDown: Move focus down in a menu
keyboardMap.row.menuMoveUp: Move focus up in a menu
keyboardMap.row.detachWorker: Detach from an attached worker session
chat.welcome: Try: "list workers", "create worker w1", "tell w1 to run tests", "status", "close w1".
chat.empty: No messages yet. Type below to talk to the worker.
workerDetail.font.label: Font size
workerDetail.composer.mergeTitle: Run pre-merge checks and merge this worker's branch into main
workerDetail.keys.heading: Keys
empty.workerDetail.title: Worker detail
empty.workerDetail.description: Select a worker from the sidebar to view details.
help.title: C4 help
help.globalIntro: C4 runs a local daemon that spawns Claude Code workers inside git worktrees. The web UI is a thin shell over the c4 CLI. Every page in Features mirrors a c4 <cmd> you could run in a terminal.
help.featureNav: Use the Features tab to open the feature sidebar. Categories: Operations (day-to-day), Automation (plan/morning/auto), Cost (token/quota), Config (templates/profiles/config/workspaces), Diagnostics (health/validation/risk).
help.shortcutHint: Press ? any time to open the keyboard shortcut sheet.
help.cliMapping: Most pages show their CLI equivalent in the banner so you can switch to the terminal when a flow does not fit the UI.
tour.step1.title: Welcome to C4
tour.step1.body: Every CLI feature has a matching page here. Use the top tabs to switch between Workers, Meetings, Specialists, Wiki, Autonomous, Sessions, History, Workflows, Features and Settings.
tour.step2.title: Feature categories
tour.step2.body: The sidebar groups features into Operations, Automation, Cost, Config and Diagnostics. Use the filter input at the top to narrow the list, then click a page to open it.
tour.step3.title: Banners describe each page
tour.step3.body: Every page opens with a short summary, the matching c4 CLI command and Learn more, so you always know what the page does.
tour.step4.title: Help and shortcuts
tour.step4.body: Press ? for shortcuts, or click the Help icon in the header to open the help drawer. You can restart this tour from Settings.
shortcuts.heading: Keyboard shortcuts
scribe.summary: Session context recorder. Tails worker + manager JSONL into docs/session-context.md so the next session picks up where the last one left off.
scribe.example: Click Start, then Scan once your session has activity. Stop writes the final snapshot and releases the timer.
scribe.useCases: Keep context across /compact boundaries.|Share a live snapshot with reviewers without pasting terminal output.|Debug why a manager lost earlier decisions.
scribe.tooltip.start: Begin the scribe timer. Safe to run more than once.
scribe.tooltip.stop: Stop the scribe timer. The last snapshot is kept.
scribe.tooltip.scan: Force an immediate scan without waiting for the interval.
scribe.tooltip.refresh: Re-fetch the scribe status and the latest context snapshot.
scribe.empty: No context snapshot yet. Click Start and then Scan to produce the first snapshot, or run c4 scribe start in a terminal.
plan.toast.dispatched: Planner dispatched — result will appear once the worker finishes.
plan.confirmRedispatch: Re-dispatch the generated plan to {worker} as a real task?
templates.toast.notImplemented: Add/remove templates — not implemented yet (server-side endpoint pending).
auto.confirmDispatch: Spawn an autonomous manager + scribe for this task? It will run with no further human approval until it finishes or halts.
specialists.confirmAuditRotate:
Rotate the specialist audit log?
Moves the JSONL to a timestamped archive.
batch.summary: Fan out a single task to N identical workers, or send one task per pasted line, in one dispatch. Returns a per-worker results table.
batch.example: Paste five tasks, pick a profile, click Dispatch - each non-comment line spawns its own worker.
batch.exampleMulti:
# one task per line
fix lint errors in src/
add tests for auth.js
update CHANGELOG for last release
batch.useCases: Apply the same refactor across several worktrees in parallel.|Migrate a file set with one task per worker.|Stress-test a slow code path with identical workers.
batch.tooltip.dispatch: Send the task(s) to the daemon. Each task gets its own worker.
batch.tooltip.modeCount: Send the same task to N workers.
batch.tooltip.modeFile: Send one task per non-comment line.
batch.tooltip.autoMode: Enable autonomous mode on each spawned worker.
batch.tooltip.prefix: Worker name prefix. batch -> batch-1, batch-2...
batch.tooltip.branch: Optional branch-name prefix. Defaults to c4/<name>.
batch.tooltip.profile: Permission profile to apply to every spawned worker.
batch.toast.failures: Batch finished with failures: {ok} ok, {fail} failed
batch.error.noTaskLine: Paste at least one non-comment line in Tasks.
plan.error.selectWorker: Select a worker and enter a task.
cleanup.summary: Remove orphan c4/ branches, worktrees and directories left behind by crashed workers or prior runs. Dry-run runs first so you can preview before committing.
cleanup.example: Open the page - the first load is already a dry-run. Review the three lists, then click Clean up to remove them.
cleanup.useCases: After a daemon crash left worktrees behind.|Before a release, to drop stale c4/ branches.|To recover disk space from old batch runs.
cleanup.tooltip.dryRun: List what would be removed without touching anything on disk.
cleanup.tooltip.commit: Remove every listed item. Always previews first.
cleanup.empty: Nothing to clean up. All workers and worktrees are reconciled.
cleanup.preview.heading: The following items will be removed
swarm.summary: Pick a root worker to see the tree of sub-workers it spawned, with each node's live status and branch.
swarm.example: Select an autonomous manager (for example, c4-mgr-auto) to view its spawned workers as a tree.
swarm.useCases: Follow an autonomous manager's progress in real time.|Find which sub-worker halted under a manager.|Audit the branch each sub-worker owns.
swarm.tooltip.refresh: Refetch the swarm for the selected root worker.
swarm.tooltip.root: Root worker whose swarm to display.
swarm.empty: No swarm data for this worker. Only managers that spawn sub-workers have a swarm.
tokenUsage.summary: Per-worker and per-day token spend alongside the live tier quota - spot runaway loops or budget a batch against today's cap.
tokenUsage.example: Switch to the 7-day range to see which worker burned the most tokens this week.
tokenUsage.useCases: Find the worker driving today's spend.|Confirm a manager is not stuck in a retry loop.|Check how close the tier is to its daily cap.
tokenUsage.tooltip.perTask: Include a per-task breakdown (slower, more data).
tokenUsage.empty: No usage recorded yet. Send a task first.
plan.summary: Dispatch a planning task, render the returned plan.md, and promote it to real work on the same worker in one click.
plan.example: Type 'Refactor ControlPanel into smaller cards', dispatch to a planner, then promote the returned plan to a real task.
plan.useCases: Turn a vague goal into a concrete step list.|Validate an approach before a senior worker picks it up.|Capture a migration as a markdown plan.
plan.tooltip.dispatch: Send the planning task to the worker. The worker writes plan.md when done.
plan.tooltip.redispatch: Re-send the generated plan to the same worker as a real task.
plan.tooltip.refresh: Reload plan.md from disk for the selected worker.
plan.tooltip.branch: Optional branch the plan worker should use.
plan.tooltip.output: Optional output path for the plan markdown.
plan.empty: No plan generated yet. Fill Task, click Send plan, then refresh when the worker finishes.
morning.summary: Daily project digest - yesterday's activity, open TODO rows, and token spend rendered as one shareable markdown report.
morning.example: Open Morning at 9am, click Generate, copy the markdown, and paste into your standup channel.
morning.useCases: Oncall handoff between shifts.|Catch up after time off.|Drop into a standup channel.
morning.tooltip.generate: Ask the daemon to regenerate the report now.
morning.tooltip.copy: Copy the raw markdown to the clipboard for pasting.
morning.empty: No report generated yet. Click Generate to produce today's report.
auto.summary: Spawn an autonomous manager plus scribe pair for a single high-level task. The manager creates its own workers, dispatches work and reports back.
auto.example: Add a JSON log endpoint to daemon.js, including tests and docs
auto.useCases: Long-running multi-step work you want to resume tomorrow.|Exploratory tasks where the plan is not yet known.|Unattended overnight work with scribe context.
auto.tooltip.dispatch: Spawn the manager. It will run with no further approval until it halts or finishes.
auto.tooltip.name: Optional name for the manager. Defaults to auto-mgr-<timestamp>.
auto.scenario.heading: Typical scenarios
auto.scenario.overnight: Overnight refactor: describe the outcome, ship in the morning.
auto.scenario.triage: Triage backlog: let the manager fan out small fixes.
auto.scenario.spike: Spike a design: the manager plans and prototypes iteratively.
templates.summary: Reusable worker templates - name, model, effort, and profile presets applied with c4 new --template <name>.
templates.example: Browse the built-in templates here; for now edit .c4/templates.json by hand to add or remove rows.
templates.useCases: Standardize a planner-implementer worker pair.|Apply an org preset without remembering flags.|Onboard teammates with vetted defaults.
templates.tooltip.filter: Filter by name, model or description.
templates.empty: No templates defined yet. Edit .c4/templates.json or run c4 templates --init.
profiles.summary: Permission profiles - allow/deny matrices a worker inherits via --profile <name>, keeping destructive tools off by default.
profiles.example: Expand the web profile to see which bash commands it allows versus denies at runtime.
profiles.useCases: Keep sensitive workers read-only.|Restrict a worker to a single directory.|Share a profile across teams for consistent defaults.
profiles.empty: No profiles defined yet. Edit .c4/profiles.json or run c4 profiles --init.
profiles.toast.notImplemented: Profile add/edit — not implemented yet (server-side endpoint pending).
health.summary: Daemon heartbeat at a glance - uptime, worker counts, queue depth, and loaded modules. First stop when something feels off.
health.example: If workers look stuck, check whether daemon uptime is suspiciously fresh - a silent restart drops live state.
health.useCases: Sanity-check the daemon after an upgrade.|Confirm worker counts match c4 list.|Watch queue depth during a heavy batch run.
health.tooltip.refresh: Refetch the health payload. Auto-refreshes every 10 seconds.
validation.summary: Per-worker validation snapshot - tests, typecheck and lint from each worktree's .c4-validation.json. Use before merging.
validation.example: Open the page before c4 merge to confirm each worker is green.
validation.useCases: Pre-merge gate across many workers.|Confirm a worker finished cleanly before close.|Spot the one worker blocking a release.
validation.empty: No workers to validate. Create one with c4 new <name> first.
